13.notes: Useful as an uplifting and nostalgic mainstream anchor, but should be paired carefully because familiarity can overpower the intended tone signal.

62.notes: High-value anchor for experimental, philosophical, and slow-cinema tolerance. Best used when the pair is explicitly testing patience or abstraction.

70.notes: Reliable dark tearjerker anchor for emotionally heavy realism, though awards familiarity can blur whether the user is choosing tone or reputation.

120.title_en: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

121.title_en: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers

122.title_en: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

149.notes: Foundational anime-canon coverage for urban dread, kinetic spectacle, and youth revolt. Important because it gives the animation lane a harder sci-fi and countercultural edge.

155.notes: Core mainstream anchor with unusually broad familiarity. Keep in rotation because it quickly stabilizes the accessible spectacle end of Phase 1.

194.notes: Useful non-English warmth anchor for image-forward romantic uplift. Helps broaden Phase 1 without relying on austere festival titles only.

310.notes: Essential Tarkovsky reference for metaphysical slowness and spiritual uncertainty. Keeps the pool's philosophical-cinema lane from becoming too plot-driven.

311.title_en: Once Upon a Time in America

346.notes: Important Kurosawa coverage for ensemble heroics, moral rigor, and action-classic lineage. Valuable as a non-Western epic canon anchor even when not frequently surfaced.

400.notes: Important Haneke-adjacent late-life intimacy reference for emotional austerity and unsentimental tenderness. Useful for keeping the international emotional lane mature rather than melodramatic.

406.notes: Key French urban-angst and social-critique reference. It keeps political volatility and street-level tension visible in the international lane.

445.notes: Important Haneke coverage for dread-through-observation and bourgeois guilt. Useful as a colder, more withholding counterpart to overt thriller entries.

508.notes: Classic international nostalgia anchor that carries cinephilia itself as a subject. Helpful for keeping warmth and memory in the non-English lane.

548.notes: Classic Kurosawa reference for subjectivity, testimony, and moral ambiguity. Valuable because it grounds non-Western narrative experimentation in foundational film history.

637.notes: Important for the bittersweet uplift lane in international cinema, though its tonal split can blur whether the user is reacting to sentiment or historical framing.

671.title_en: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone

797.notes: Essential Bergman coverage for psychological fracture and modernist identity play. Keep as a reference point for users whose taste leans toward austere formal intensity.

840.title_en: Close Encounters of the Third Kind

901.title_en: The Diving Bell and the Butterfly

939.notes: Foundational Wong Kar-wai anchor for restrained longing, visual elegance, and nostalgia. Important because it carries both cinephile legitimacy and broad emotional readability.

1111.notes: Important J-horror reference for dread by atmosphere rather than gore. Helps preserve a quieter supernatural-horror lane beside louder psychological entries.

1396.notes: High-value Tarkovsky entry for memory logic and spiritual abstraction. Best kept as depth coverage rather than a frequently surfaced first-contact title.

1427.title_en: Perfume: The Story of a Murderer

2017.notes: Important Bong Joon-ho coverage for procedural unease, institutional failure, and tonal instability. Keeps Korean crime cinema from being represented only by twist-heavy or prestige titles.

2346.notes: Strong cinephile anchor for ruin-poetics, social critique, and slow observation. Important for signaling that the system understands non-mainstream taste early.

4977.notes: Important Satoshi Kon coverage for dream-logic animation and identity slippage. Helps preserve a distinct animation lane beyond either family warmth or pure horror.

9323.notes: Core anime-cinephile reference for philosophical cyberpunk and visual immersion. Valuable as a bridge between contemplative sci-fi and animation-focused taste.

10195.title_en: The Girl Who Leapt Through Time

10494.notes: High-impact animation anchor for psychologically intense viewers. It is especially valuable because it separates animation fandom from family-animation assumptions.

10515.notes: Useful Miyazaki coverage for adventure warmth and airborne wonder. It keeps animation breadth from leaning too heavily toward only melancholy or destabilizing titles.

10687.title_en: Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter... and Spring
10687.notes: Important Korean contemplative-cinema reference for cyclical spirituality and visual restraint. It broadens Korean coverage beyond crime, horror, and social pressure.

10835.notes: High-value Wong Kar-wai coverage for urban loneliness, impulsive romance, and pop restlessness. Important as a looser counterpart to the restraint of In the Mood for Love.

11090.notes: Useful Kore-eda family-drama entry for everyday disappointment and gentle observation. It gives the Japanese lane a domestic realism register distinct from transcendence or mood-piece abstraction.

11220.notes: Useful for Wong Kar-wai's nocturnal alienation and jagged energy. It preserves the more abrasive side of his cinema instead of only the elegant-romantic register.

11534.notes: Important companion piece to Uncle Boonmee for Apichatpong's sensual, elusive mode. Useful when the pool needs a more intimate form of dreamlike slowness.

11694.title_en: The Diving Bell and the Butterfly

11985.notes: Key Tsai Ming-liang entry for urban alienation and slow formalism. Keep as a deliberate Phase 1 marker of arthouse patience and loneliness.

12444.title_en: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows Part 2

14626.notes: Keeps the pool's sensorial European formalism from collapsing into only nostalgia-coded or dialogue-heavy entries. A key art-cinema body-language reference point.

15899.notes: Important Hou Hsiao-hsien coverage for historical memory and Taiwanese New Cinema lineage. Keep for breadth even if it is not a frequent sequencing surface title.

16306.notes: Useful cult-animation outlier for surreal world-building and alienation. Protects the pool from treating animation only as Japanese or family-facing.

16662.notes: Important Varda coverage for feminine subjectivity, urban drift, and formal lightness. Helps keep the European arthouse lane from becoming too severe or masculine.

16672.notes: Strong classic-art-cinema reference for existential entrapment and tactile formalism. Important for older non-Western canon coverage.

17431.notes: Useful French crime-art-cinema bridge for ambition, violence, and institutional pressure. It gives the pool a tougher international crime register beyond canon classics.

18329.notes: Key Wong Kar-wai addition that widens the romantic and queer-cinema lane without softening the emotional volatility. Useful as a more restless counterpart to In the Mood for Love.

18491.title_en: Neon Genesis Evangelion: The End of Evangelion

22429.notes: Important Ang Lee family-drama coverage for intergenerational warmth, ritual, and emotional restraint. It provides a more accessible Taiwanese counterweight to the New Cinema auteurs.

25237.notes: Important anti-war extremity reference for traumatic intensity and moral horror. Best kept as depth coverage rather than a frequently surfaced taste probe.

25538.notes: Essential Yang anchor for humane ensemble observation and modern family sprawl. It gives the pool a patient, emotionally precise counterweight to more stylized auteurs.

30017.notes: Important Kiarostami entry for docu-fiction ambiguity and self-reflexive realism. Valuable for serious cinephile coverage even if too rarefied for frequent pairing.

30020.notes: Keeps the Iranian existential lane explicit in the pool. Best used as deep coverage for viewers who consistently favor contemplative moral and philosophical cinema.

33320.notes: Useful bridge title between cinephile animation and emotional visual storytelling. Helps keep animation coverage from collapsing into either child-facing or purely dark entries.

37903.notes: Important Haneke period reference for repression, cruelty, and cold social order. Useful for the severe historical-art-cinema lane.

38368.title_en: Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives
38368.notes: Crucial Apichatpong coverage for spiritual drift, rural stillness, and dreamlike patience. It is one of the pool's clearest signals that Southeast Asian arthouse cinema matters here.

44012.title_en: Jeanne Dielman, 23, quai du Commerce, 1080 Bruxelles
44012.notes: High-value Akerman reference for extreme routine, duration, and domestic formalism. Important as a canon marker even if it remains a low-frequency sequencing candidate.

45935.notes: Adds a denser, more interior Hou mode than The Assassin. Useful for preserving historical visual-poise coverage without defaulting to Western period prestige.

45999.title_en: A Time to Live, a Time to Die
45999.notes: Important autobiographical Hou coverage for memory, family, and Taiwanese historical texture. Strengthens the pool's non-Western coming-of-age depth.

48303.notes: Important Kiarostami coverage for ambiguity in intimacy and identity performance. It broadens the Iranian lane beyond explicitly existential road or rural cinema.

49084.notes: High-value Lee Chang-dong anchor for moral attention, social pain, and lyrical restraint. Useful when the system needs a humane, emotionally precise arthouse counterweight.

57564.notes: Key Tsai Ming-liang entry for youth alienation and urban drift. Useful as a sharper, more restless counterpart to Vive L'Amour in the same auteur lane.

60243.notes: Critical non-English anchor for dialogue, moral ambiguity, and social realism. Important for keeping the pool's serious-realist lane internationally grounded.

76170.notes: Useful Bong Joon-ho bridge title between accessible genre energy and overt class allegory. It helps the international lane keep momentum without becoming purely prestige-oriented.

78450.notes: Important Yang coverage for fractured urban narratives and emotional distance. Gives the Taiwanese New Cinema lane more than a single family-epic reference.

82695.title_en: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

83201.notes: Important Latin American political-cinema reference for media strategy, irony, and democratic performance. It adds a lighter-toned but still serious political register.

87414.notes: Important Scandinavian moral-pressure drama reference. Useful for keeping social-panic realism in the pool outside Anglophone prestige cinema.

88811.notes: Useful for preserving the drifting, low-key criminal underside of Hou's cinema. It expands the pool's texture beyond stately prestige selections.

119049.notes: Important Polish austerity-and-memory reference for spare black-and-white spiritual searching. It strengthens the pool's compact formalist lane.

149871.title_en: The Tale of the Princess Kaguya
149871.notes: Important animation-canon coverage for hand-drawn fragility, melancholy, and classical storytelling. It widens the pool's animation lane beyond techno-surreal entries.

157336.notes: Reliable broad-spectrum spectacle anchor that still carries philosophical and emotional weight. Useful when the contrast needs a recognizable large-scale film with sincerity.

187022.notes: Keeps Jia Zhangke's sharper and more violent social-critique mode in the pool. Useful as a counterweight to more observational Chinese arthouse selections.

253450.notes: High-value Hou Hsiao-hsien anchor for image-driven stillness and formal distance. Essential to the upgraded pool because it sharpens the gaze-versus-momentum contrast.

265228.notes: Critical Africa coverage for moral pressure, restraint, and political violence. Important because it prevents the international lane from being dominated only by Europe and East Asia.

293670.notes: Important Korean horror-cinephile anchor for folk dread, dread accumulation, and tonal instability. Keeps horror coverage from collapsing into only US cult references.

301528.notes: Strong Park Chan-wook coverage for erotic tension, deception, and ornate visual control. Useful as a more baroque complement to the colder Korean entries.

315837.notes: Important anime-mainstream bridge for emotion, fantasy, and youth longing. It helps the animation lane speak to both cinephile and broader audiences.

336808.notes: Key Latin American coverage for spiritual journey, colonial violence, and black-and-white visual austerity. Important for extending the pool beyond Euro-American art-cinema defaults.

354275.notes: Important Hong Sang-soo entry for repetition, conversational variation, and emotional self-revision. It defines a quieter authorial lane that would otherwise be missing.

392044.notes: Important animation coverage for shame, repair, and adolescent emotional injury. It widens the Japanese animation lane beyond fantasy and mind-bending entries.

413594.notes: Useful Shinkai coverage for romantic weather-melancholy and contemporary urban fantasy. It gives the animation lane a glossy emotional-modern register.

434405.notes: Important contemporary Taiwan coverage for family rupture, masculinity, and quiet despair. It keeps the Taiwanese lane contemporary rather than purely canonical.

434773.title_en: On the Beach at Night Alone
434773.notes: Important Hong Sang-soo coverage for conversational intimacy, repetition, and emotional self-exposure. It helps define a quieter dialogue-driven auteur lane.

441282.title_en: Night Is Short Walk on Girl

459581.notes: Useful Taiwan coverage for queer family melodrama with sharper emotional volatility. It broadens the region's representation beyond austere slow cinema.

486898.notes: Important Bi Gan coverage for memory drift and oneiric visual immersion. It keeps the pool's Chinese arthouse lane contemporary rather than purely canonical.

490125.notes: Important Middle Eastern social-realist coverage for childhood precarity and institutional neglect. Keeps the humanitarian-realist lane visible beyond Europe and East Asia.

491584.notes: Strong anchor for ambiguous menace, class anxiety, and slow psychological burn. One of the most important new films for the cinephile-facing opening pairs.

496243.notes: Now widely familiar, but still a high-value bridge between accessibility and sharper social critique. Keep as a cross-audience anchor, not as the only Korean representative.

517814.title_en: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
517814.notes: Key formal-romantic anchor for slowburn visual intimacy. Helps define the contemplative end of the pool without defaulting to heterosexual nostalgia coding.

614696.notes: Important Korean diasporic family-drama coverage for tenderness, labor, and intergenerational tension. It adds a quieter immigrant perspective absent from the harsher Korean entries.

630566.notes: Important Hamaguchi anchor for dialogue-rich existential patience. It deepens the pool's contemporary festival canon without leaning on visual maximalism.
